title: Use of nonexistent operators with 'like' syntax
description: Avoid the use of nonexistent operators with 'like' syntax as they can lead to unexpected behavior.
message: Use of 'like' syntax with nonexistent operator
recommendation: Replace with a valid operator or use a different comparison method

compliant.code: if any(item.endswith('d') for item in some_list):
compliant.description: Using a list comprehension and 'endswith' method instead of 'like'

why_is_this_an_issue: The 'like' operator is intended for string patterns, using it with nonexistent operators can lead to unintended behavior and bugs.
how_to_fix: Replace the use of the 'like' operator with a valid operator or a different comparison method.

logic.new_check_type_doc: Checks for the use of the 'like' operator with nonexistent operator.

metadata.title: Use of nonexistent operators with 'like' syntax
